PERMISSION TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF JACKSON SR, RAYMOND D C-27148/V-243

DISCOVERY 302, THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING ON PLAINTIFF RAYMOND JACKSON'S MOTION FOR COURT ORDER GRANTING LEAVE TO TAKE DEFENDANT'S DEPOSITION BY OTHER THAN THAT OF STENO-GRAPHIC MEANS AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S UNOPPOSED MOTION IS GRANTED. THE DEPOSITION MAY PROCEED AT CMF IN VACAVILLE PURSUANT TO THE PROCEDURES SET FORTH IN PLAINTIFF'S MOVING PAPERS. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER TO JUDGE PRO TEM TONY SCHOENBERG FOR REVIEW AND SIGNATURE. JUDGE PRO TEM: TONY SCHOENBERG, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, MATTER NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Judicial Notice That A Serious Break-Down In The Delivery Of Plntf'S Second Request For An Extention Of Time In The Amount Of 30 Days Was Erroneously Not Received By This Court And No Fault Of Plntf'S Ask He Timely Submitted The Second Extension Of Time In This Case And Any Motion For Dismissal Must Be Stricken
Off Calendar Sep-27-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Raymond Jackson, Plaintiff in Pro Per appears telephonically through Court's phone.
Kevin Martin, Esq. 510-444-7600 of Martin APC for Defendant Paul Martin through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court addresses Plaintiff, Mr. Jackson's concern re: the letter sent by the Court on August 6, 2018. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF RAYMOND JACKSON's Motion Of Judicial Notice That A Serious Break-Down In The Delivery Of Plaintiff's Second Request For An Extention Of Time In The Amount Of 30 Days Was Erroneously Not Received By This Court And No Fault Of Plaintiff's Ask He Timely Submitted The Second Extension Of Time In This Case And Any Motion For Dismissal Must Be Stricken.

Off calendar. The Court will inform Judge Jackson that a Second Amended complaint is properly on file. The case may move forward with Discovery.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-06-2018 Continued To Oct-17-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-07-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Raymond D Jackson Sr., Plaintiff appearing in pro-per by telephone appearance;
No other appearances on the matter.



RULING - The matter was discussed. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Paul Martin's demurrer to the entirety of the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Raymond Jackson is sustained with 45 days leave to amend until July 23, 2018. No opposition filed. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR # 8600 =302/hk

Off Calendar Apr-25-2018 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF RAYMOND JACKSON'S Motion To Amended The First Amended Complaint is OFF CALENDAR without prejudice to filing a motion for leave to file a second amended complaint with a proof of service showing that the defendants were served with the motion papers. No proof of service filed.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: not reported (L&M302)

Notice Of Motion For Court Order Granting Leave To Take Defendant'S Deposition By Other Than That Of Steno-Graphic Means
Off Calendar Apr-17-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance by any party or counsel.

There being no appearances, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on Plaintiff Raymond Jackson's Motion For Court Order Granting Leave To Take Defendant's Deposition By Other Than That Of Steno-Graphic Means as follows: Plaintiff's unopposed motion is granted. The deposition may proceed at CMF in Vacaville pursuant to the procedures set forth in plaintiff's moving papers. The prevailing party is to submit a proposed form of order to Judge Pro Tem Tony Schoenberg for review and signature.

Judge Pro Tem: Tony Schoenberg, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Matter Not Reported. =(302/JPT/HEK)
